    30→

    32→

    34→- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_511` (snake_case matching namespace number).
    35→- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.

    41→
    42→The problem asks a yes/no question ("Is it true that, for every $c > 1$, the set ... has at most $O_c(1)$ many connected components of diameter $> c$?"), so **Case 1** applies.
    43→

    46→- [x] `answer(sorry)` replaced with `answer(False)` since the conjecture was disproved.
    47→- [x] All quantifiers (`∀ c`, `∀ f`, `∀ k`, etc.) appear on the RHS of `↔`, not as theorem parameters.

    52→- [x] Value is `research solved` — appropriate since Pommerenke disproved the conjecture.
    53→- [x] Not `research open`, so no constraint on `sorry`-free proofs.

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/511.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 511`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/511](https://www.erdosproblems.com/511)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (monic polynomials and connected components of sublevel sets in the complex plane).
- [x] Optional bibliography: mentions Pommerenke's disproof but no formal citation tags. Not required.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos511 ... end Erdos511`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos511`.
- [x] `open Polynomial Set` appears **before** the namespace block, as required for Mathlib namespaces.

## 5. Custom Definitions

No custom `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file. N/A — all items pass vacuously.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_511` (snake_case matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 30]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`) describing the conjecture and its resolution.
- [ ] Docstring references source papers: mentions Pommerenke by name but does not use formal bibliography tags (e.g., `[Po??]`). **Minor — "should ideally" per checklist, not a hard requirement.**

### `answer()` usage

The problem asks a yes/no question ("Is it true that, for every $c > 1$, the set ... has at most $O_c(1)$ many connected components of diameter $> c$?"), so **Case 1** applies.

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(False) ↔ P` — correct for a yes/no question answered negatively (disproved by Pommerenke).
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked (the positive claim that such a uniform bound exists), so `answer(False)` correctly means "no".
- [x] `answer(sorry)` replaced with `answer(False)` since the conjecture was disproved.
- [x] All quantifiers (`∀ c`, `∀ f`, `∀ k`, etc.) appear on the RHS of `↔`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research solved` — appropriate since Pommerenke disproved the conjecture.
- [x] Not `research open`, so no constraint on `sorry`-free proofs.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 30`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (one: 30).
- [x] Only one number, so ascending order is trivially satisfied.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] 30 is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Functions of a complex variable).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses multi-line `by` / `sorry` form (`:= by\n  sorry`), which is acceptable per checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in any `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` (none exist).
- [x] No informal proof sketch included.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

No variants present. N/A — no items to check.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout (continuation lines in the theorem statement use consistent indentation).
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `ℂ`, `∈`, `⊆`, `→`, `↔`, `≠`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run as part of this review. **Must be verified before merge.**
- [ ] Custom linter pass not independently verified.

---

## Summary

**Status: PASS (with minor notes)**

The file is well-structured and conforms to essentially all checklist requirements. Two observations:

1. **Docstring bibliography (§6):** The theorem docstring names Pommerenke but does not use a formal citation tag. This is a soft recommendation ("should ideally"), not a blocking issue.
2. **Build verification (§12):** Not tested in this review — must be confirmed in CI.

No blocking issues found.

    16→
    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 511
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/511](https://www.erdosproblems.com/511)
    23→
    24→Let $f(z) \in \mathbb{C}[z]$ be a monic polynomial of degree $n$. Is it true that,
    25→for every $c > 1$, the set $\{z \in \mathbb{C} : |f(z)| < 1\}$ has at most $O_c(1)$
    26→many connected components of diameter $> c$ (where the implied constant is
    27→independent of $n$)?
    28→
    29→Disproved by Pommerenke, who showed that for any $0 < d < 4$ and $k \geq 1$,
    30→there exist monic polynomials $f \in \mathbb{C}[x]$ such that
    31→$\{z : |f(z)| \leq 1\}$ has at least $k$ connected components of diameter $\geq d$.
    32→-/
    33→
    34→open Polynomial Set
    35→
    36→namespace Erdos511
    37→
    38→/-- Erdős Problem 511: Is it true that for every $c > 1$, there exists a bound $M$
    39→(independent of the degree) such that for any monic polynomial $f \in \mathbb{C}[z]$,
    40→the sublevel set $\{z \in \mathbb{C} : \|f(z)\| < 1\}$ has at most $M$ connected
    41→components of diameter $> c$?
    42→
    43→Disproved by Pommerenke. -/
    44→@[category research solved, AMS 30]
    45→theorem erdos_511 : answer(False) ↔
    46→    ∀ c : ℝ, c > 1 →
    47→      ∃ M : ℕ, ∀ (f : Polynomial ℂ),
    48→        f.Monic →
    49→          ∀ (k : ℕ) (C : Fin k → Set ℂ),
    50→            (∀ i, (C i).Nonempty) →
    51→            (∀ i, C i ⊆ {z : ℂ | ‖Polynomial.eval z f‖ < 1}) →
    52→            (∀ i, IsPreconnected (C i)) →
    53→            (∀ i, Metric.diam (C i) > c) →
    54→            (∀ i j, i ≠ j → Disjoint (C i) (C j)) →
    55→            k ≤ M := by
    56→  sorry
    57→
    58→end Erdos511
    59→

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 511

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/511](https://www.erdosproblems.com/511)

Let $f(z) \in \mathbb{C}[z]$ be a monic polynomial of degree $n$. Is it true that,
for every $c > 1$, the set $\{z \in \mathbb{C} : |f(z)| < 1\}$ has at most $O_c(1)$
many connected components of diameter $> c$ (where the implied constant is
independent of $n$)?

Disproved by Pommerenke, who showed that for any $0 < d < 4$ and $k \geq 1$,
there exist monic polynomials $f \in \mathbb{C}[x]$ such that
$\{z : |f(z)| \leq 1\}$ has at least $k$ connected components of diameter $\geq d$.
-/

open Polynomial Set

namespace Erdos511

/-- Erdős Problem 511: Is it true that for every $c > 1$, there exists a bound $M$
(independent of the degree) such that for any monic polynomial $f \in \mathbb{C}[z]$,
the sublevel set $\{z \in \mathbb{C} : \|f(z)\| < 1\}$ has at most $M$ connected
components of diameter $> c$?

Disproved by Pommerenke. -/
@[category research solved, AMS 30]
theorem erdos_511 : answer(False) ↔
    ∀ c : ℝ, c > 1 →
      ∃ M : ℕ, ∀ (f : Polynomial ℂ),
        f.Monic →
          ∀ (k : ℕ) (C : Fin k → Set ℂ),
            (∀ i, (C i).Nonempty) →
            (∀ i, C i ⊆ {z : ℂ | ‖Polynomial.eval z f‖ < 1}) →
            (∀ i, IsPreconnected (C i)) →
            (∀ i, Metric.diam (C i) > c) →
            (∀ i j, i ≠ j → Disjoint (C i) (C j)) →
            k ≤ M := by
  sorry

end Erdos511

content.text: The review status is **PASS**. No changes needed.
